category_reason: system/internal signal or no positively user-visible signal (expected All 10 'test concurrent request handling' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour, observed [api-tester-test-concurrent-request-handling] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0)
sub_test: Aggregate failure: test concurrent request handling (10/10 scenario checks failed)
title: [test-concurrent-request-handling] Aggregate failure: test concurrent request handling (10/10 scenario checks failed) on n/a — aggregate finding across endpoints — expected All 10 'test concurrent request handling' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour, observed [api-tester-test-concurrent-request-handling] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0 (undocumented)
expected: All 10 'test concurrent request handling' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour

testing_steps: 1. Start the target API at http://localhost:8899.

2. Write a concurrency script (Python asyncio+aiohttp, or `seq 20 | xargs -P20 -I_ curl -s -o /tmp/r_.json -w '%{http_code}\n' http://localhost:8899/products`) that fires 20 identical GET http://localhost:8899/products requests in parallel.
3. Assert all 20 responses carry the same status code and byte-identical bodies.

4. Repeat with 20 concurrent PUT http://localhost:8899/products/1 requests carrying distinct payloads, then GET /products/1 once — the final state must equal exactly one sent payload (no merged/torn write).
5. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/test-concurrent-request-handling/cases.json — this run recorded 10 of 10 checks failing.

description: The test-concurrent-request-handling check failed in aggregate against http://localhost:8899: [api-tester-test-concurrent-request-handling] quality outcome=FAIL; metric=0.0. There is no single reproducible API call — reproduce it with the script described in the steps below (which is also why this bug has no Postman section).

2. Write a concurrency script (Python asyncio+aiohttp, or `seq 20 | xargs -P20 -I_ curl -s -o /tmp/r_.json -w '%{http_code}\n' http://localhost:8899/products`) that fires 20 identical GET http://localhost:8899/products requests in parallel.
3. Assert all 20 responses carry the same status code and byte-identical bodies.

4. Repeat with 20 concurrent PUT http://localhost:8899/products/1 requests carrying distinct payloads, then GET /products/1 once — the final state must equal exactly one sent payload (no merged/torn write).
5. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/test-concurrent-request-handling/cases.json — this run recorded 10 of 10 checks failing.
